summary: File-level verification of supabase migration 007_b9_demo_seed.sql (pgcrypto-dependency fix). 33/35 pytest checks pass. Critical finding: the bcrypt-shaped placeholder string written to auth.users.encrypted_password is 65 characters long, NOT 60 — violating both the user's explicit stated invariant ('exactly 60 characters long') and the canonical bcrypt shape `$2[axyb]$NN$<53 chars>` (60 total). All other invariants verified: no unqualified crypt(/gen_salt( calls remain, no CREATE EXTENSION pgcrypto, SQL is well-formed (balanced $$ and parens, sqlparse-parseable, semicolon-terminated), migration is idempotent (auth.users uses `on conflict (id) do nothing`; all 5 profile_* subtables use `on conflict do nothing`; verification_status uses `on conflict (profile_id) do nothing`; profiles use lookup-then-update pattern), gen_random_uuid() remains in seed_demo_conversations_for_me (Postgres 13+ built-in, no pgcrypto needed), all 6 demo uuids passed to _seed_demo_user() match those referenced in the RPC's user_id IN(...) list, and `grant execute ... to authenticated` is present. Flutter side untouched: `flutter analyze --no-fatal-infos` on /app/nextchapter_audit/src reports 0 errors / 0 warnings / 78 accepted info-level deprecations.

critical.issue: Placeholder bcrypt string `$2a$10$DemoAccountNoLoginXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXX` is 65 characters long, not 60. Standard bcrypt is exactly 60 chars: `$2[axyb]$NN$` (7-char header) + 53-char body. The current string has a 58-char body (18 chars 'DemoAccountNoLogin' + 40 X's). The user's own testing criteria explicitly require length==60. Fix: drop 5 X's so the body is 53 chars total (e.g. 18 char 'DemoAccountNoLogin' + 35 X's → `$2a$10$DemoAccountNoLoginXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXX`, total 60).

action_items: Trim the placeholder on line 63 of 007_b9_demo_seed.sql from 65 chars to exactly 60 chars by removing 5 of the trailing X's. Suggested replacement (60 chars): `$2a$10$DemoAccountNoLoginXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXX` — i.e. 7-char `$2a$10$` header + 18-char 'DemoAccountNoLogin' + 35 X's = 60. This is a one-character-count edit, no functional logic change. Although Supabase's `auth.users.encrypted_password` is TEXT and won't reject 65-char strings at INSERT time, conforming to the canonical 60-char bcrypt shape avoids any future surprises (gotrue defensively validates shape on a few code paths) and matches the user's stated invariant.
critical_code_review_comments: Line 63 placeholder is 65 chars instead of 60. The body after `$2a$10$` is 58 chars (DemoAccountNoLogin = 18 + 40 X's); a real bcrypt hash has exactly 53 chars after the header (22-char base64 salt + 31-char base64 hash). User's testing criteria explicitly demand len==60. HIGH priority for the 'shape correctness' contract, LOW priority for actually-blocking-the-migration (auth.users.encrypted_password column is plain TEXT in Supabase's gotrue schema and will accept any length at insert time).

pgcrypto-dependency removal is otherwise complete and correct: zero unqualified `crypt(`/`gen_salt(` tokens anywhere in the file (verified with comment-stripped regex), no CREATE EXTENSION calls. `gen_random_uuid()` correctly retained — it is a Postgres 13+ built-in living in pg_catalog, NOT pgcrypto, and works without any extension.

Idempotency is fully wired: auth.users insert → `on conflict (id) do nothing`; profile_photos / profile_prompts / profile_interests / profile_looking_for / profile_life_situation all → `on conflict do nothing`; verification_status → `on conflict (profile_id) do nothing`; profiles handled via `select id into new_profile_id from public.profiles where user_id = in_user_id` then branch (insert if null, update otherwise). Both functions use `create or replace function`. Re-running the migration multiple times is safe.

SQL well-formedness verified: sqlparse parses ≥3 top-level statements (helper function, DO block, RPC function — the GRANT is parsed as part of the RPC group), $$ delimiters balanced (4 occurrences, even), parentheses balanced after stripping line-comments and single-quoted strings (including '' escapes), file terminates on `$$;` then a `grant execute ... ;` (the last non-empty line ends with `;`).

Demo uuid set is consistent across the file: all 6 uuids (00000000-0000-4000-8000-00000000000{1..6}) are both passed as the first arg to `public._seed_demo_user('<uuid>', ...)` inside the DO block AND listed in the `user_id in (...)` lookup inside `seed_demo_conversations_for_me`. No orphaned demo or missing demo — the seeded conversations will find all 6 partners.
`grant execute on function public.seed_demo_conversations_for_me() to authenticated;` is present on line 282 so signed-in real users can call the inbox-seeder.

Flutter side: 0 errors, 0 warnings, 78 info-level deprecations (withOpacity → withValues, RadioGroup migration, FormField.value → initialValue, plus a few new since iteration 1 — was 77, now 78, but all info-level and accepted by the user per iteration 1).

success_rate.backend: 94% (33/35 file-level checks passed; 2 failures both stem from the same 65-vs-60 char placeholder issue)
success_rate.frontend: N/A (frontend testing explicitly skipped per request; flutter analyze: 0 errors / 0 warnings / 78 accepted info deprecations)

test_credentials: Not applicable — no DB / API access required for this task.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All checks except the placeholder-length are green. After main agent trims the placeholder to exactly 60 chars (suggested string in action_items), re-run `pytest /app/backend/tests/test_migration_007_b9_demo_seed.py -v` and update EXPECTED_PLACEHOLDER in the test file to the new 60-char string. All 35 checks should then pass. No DB available in this environment; user will paste-and-run in the Supabase SQL editor.
rca of the issue: Root cause of the migration error reported by user (`ERROR: 42883: function gen_salt(unknown) does not exist`): Supabase installs the `pgcrypto` extension into the `extensions` schema rather than `public`, and the SQL Editor's default search_path does not include `extensions`. So `gen_salt('bf')` and `crypt(...)` calls inside the security-definer helper `_seed_demo_user()` (which sets `search_path = public`) cannot resolve. The fix correctly side-steps pgcrypto entirely by hardcoding a bcrypt-shaped placeholder into encrypted_password — auth.users.encrypted_password is a plain TEXT column whose shape is only validated by gotrue at login time, and demo accounts are never logged into. Residual issue introduced by the fix: the chosen placeholder is 65 chars instead of the canonical 60 (18-char 'DemoAccountNoLogin' + 40 X's after `$2a$10$`, total 65). Trim 5 X's to make it 60. The migration will still run successfully even at 65 chars (TEXT column accepts any length), so this is a contract-correctness issue, not a blocking SQL error.
